name: can-isotp

metadata.name: can-isotp
metadata.summary: Module enabling the IsoTP protocol defined by ISO-15765

documentation:
# python-can-isotp

```{image} https://app.travis-ci.com/pylessard/python-can-isotp.svg?branch=master
```

This project is a Python package meant to provide support for IsoTP (ISO-15765) protocol written in Python 3. The code is published under MIT license on GitHub ([pylessard/python-can-isotp](https://github.com/pylessard/python-can-isotp)).

This package contains a Python implementation of the protocol that works in the user space that may or may not be coupled with [python-can](https://python-can.readthedocs.io). It also contains a wrapper for a simplified usage of the [Linux SocketCAN IsoTP kernel module](https://github.com/hartkopp/can-isotp)

## Documentation

The documentation is available here : <https://can-isotp.readthedocs.io>

## Requirements

> - Python 3.7+

## Installation

using pip:

```
pip install can-isotp
```

classes.name: isotp.errors.BlockingSendFailure
documentation: Happens when a blocking send fails to complete

classes.name: isotp.errors.BadGeneratorError
documentation: Happens when the user tries to send data using a generator and provides a size that does not match the amount of data in the generator

classes.name: isotp.errors.BlockingSendTimeout
documentation: Happens when a blocking send fails to complete because the user timeout is expired. Inherits {class}`BlockingSendFailure<isotp.BlockingSendFailure>`

classes.name: isotp.errors.FlowControlTimeoutError
documentation:
Happens when the senders fails to sends a Flow Control message in time.
Refer to TransportLayer parameter {ref}`rx_flowcontrol_timeout<param_rx_flowcontrol_timeout>`

classes.name: isotp.errors.ConsecutiveFrameTimeoutError
documentation:
Happens when the senders fails to sends a Consecutive Frame message in time.
Refer to TransportLayer parameter {ref}`rx_consecutive_frame_timeout<param_rx_consecutive_frame_timeout>`

classes.name: isotp.errors.InvalidCanDataError
documentation: Happens when a CAN message that cannot be decoded as valid First Frame, Consecutive Frame, Single Frame or Flow Control PDU is received.

classes.name: isotp.errors.UnexpectedFlowControlError
documentation: Happens when a Flow Control message is received and was not expected

classes.name: isotp.errors.UnexpectedConsecutiveFrameError
documentation: Happens when a Consecutive Frame message is received and was not expected

classes.name: isotp.errors.ReceptionInterruptedWithSingleFrameError
documentation: Happens when the reception of a multi packet message reception is interrupted with a new Single Frame PDU.

classes.name: isotp.errors.ReceptionInterruptedWithFirstFrameError
documentation: Happens when the reception of a multi packet message reception is interrupted with a new First Frame PDU.

classes.name: isotp.errors.WrongSequenceNumberError
documentation: Happens when a consecutive frame is received with a wrong sequence number.

classes.name: isotp.errors.UnsupportedWaitFrameError
documentation: Happens when a Flow Control PDU with FlowStatus=Wait is received and {ref}`wftmax<param_wftmax>` is set to 0

classes.name: isotp.errors.MaximumWaitFrameReachedError
documentation: Happens when too much Flow Control PDU with FlowStatus=Wait is received. Refer to {ref}`wftmax<param_wftmax>`

classes.name: isotp.errors.FrameTooLongError
documentation: Happens when a FirstFrame with a length (FF_DL) longer than {ref}`max_frame_size<param_max_frame_size>` is received.

classes.name: isotp.errors.ChangingInvalidRXDLError
documentation: Happens when a ConsecutiveFrame is received with a length smaller than `RX_DL` (size of first frame) without being the last message of the IsoTP frame.

classes.name: isotp.errors.MissingEscapeSequenceError
documentation: Happens when a SingleFrame with length (CAN_DL) greater than 8 bytes is received and the length of the payload (SF_DL) is encoded in the first byte, which is forbidden by ISO-15765-2

classes.name: isotp.errors.InvalidCanFdFirstFrameRXDL
documentation: Happens when a FirstFrame is received with missing data; In other words when CAN_DL is smaller than the deduced RX_DL. The sender did not optimized the capacity usage of the CAN message.

classes.name: isotp.errors.OverflowError
documentation: Happens when the TransportLayer receive a FlowControl PDU with a FlowStatus=Overflow (2). In this event, the transmission is stopped.

classes.name: isotp.can_message.CanMessage
documentation:
Represent a CAN message (ISO-11898)

```
param arbitration_id

The CAN arbitration ID. Must be a 11 bits value or a 29 bits value if extended_id is True

type arbitration_id

int

param dlc

The Data Length Code representing the number of bytes in the data field

type dlc

int

param data

The 8 bytes payload of the message

type data

bytearray

param extended_id

When True, the arbitration ID stands on 29 bits. 11 bits when False

type extended_id

bool

param is_fd

When True, message has to be transmitted or has been received in a CAN FD frame. CAN frame when set to False

type is_fd

bool

param bitrate_switch

When True, message has the Bit Rate Switch (BRS) bit set.

type bitrate_switch

bool
```

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.LISTEN_MODE
documentation: Puts the socket in Listen mode, which prevents transmission of data

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.EXTEND_ADDR
documentation: When set, an address extension byte (set in socket general options) will be added to each payload sent. Unless RX_EXT_ADDR is also set, this value will be expected for reception as well

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.TX_PADDING
documentation: Enables padding of transmitted data with a byte set in the socket general options

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.RX_PADDING
documentation: Indicates that data padding is possible in reception. Must be set for CHK_PAD_LEN and CHK_PAD_DATA to have an effect

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.CHK_PAD_LEN
documentation: Makes the socket validate the padding length of the CAN message

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.CHK_PAD_DATA
documentation: Makes the socket validate the padding bytes of the CAN message

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.HALF_DUPLEX
documentation: Sets the socket in half duplex mode, forcing transmission and reception to happen sequentially

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.FORCE_TXSTMIN
documentation: Forces the socket to use the separation time sets in general options, overriding stmin value received in flow control frames.

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.FORCE_RXSTMIN
documentation: Forces the socket to ignore any message received faster than stmin given in the flow control frame

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.RX_EXT_ADDR
documentation: When sets, a different extended address can be used for reception than for transmission.

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.flags.WAIT_TX_DONE
documentation: When set, we wait for tx completion to make sure the PDU is completely passed to the CAN netdevice queue.

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.LinkLayerProtocol.CAN
documentation: Internal structure size of a CAN 2.0 frame

class_variables.name: isotp.tpsock.LinkLayerProtocol.CAN_FD
documentation: Internal structure size of a CAN FD frame

classes.name: isotp.tpsock.socket

methods.name: isotp.tpsock.socket.set_ll_opts
documentation:
Sets the link layer options. Default values are set to work with CAN 2.0. Link layer may be configure to work in CAN FD.
Values of `None` will leave the parameter unchanged

```
param mtu

The internal CAN frame structure size. Possible values are defined in 

type mtu

int

param tx_dl

The CAN message payload length. For CAN 2.0, this value should be 8. For CAN FD, possible values are 8,12,16,20,24,32,48,64

type tx_dl

int

param tx_flags

Link layer flags.

type tx_flags

int

rtype

```

methods.name: isotp.tpsock.socket.set_opts
documentation:
Sets the general options of the socket. Values of `None` will leave the parameter unchanged

```
param optflag

A list of flags modifying the protocol behavior. Refer to 

type optflag

int

param frame_txtime

Frame transmission time (N_As/N_Ar) in nanoseconds.

type frame_txtime

int

param ext_address

The extended address to use. If not None, flags.EXTEND_ADDR will be set.

type ext_address

int

param txpad

The byte to use to pad the transmitted CAN messages. If not None, flags.TX_PADDING will be set

type txpad

int

param rxpad

The byte to use to pad the transmitted CAN messages. If not None, flags.RX_PADDING will be set

type rxpad

int

param rx_ext_address

The extended address to use in reception. If not None, flags.RX_EXT_ADDR will be set

type rx_ext_address

int

param tx_stmin

Sets the transmit separation time (time between consecutive frame) in nanoseconds. This value will override the value received through FlowControl frame. If not None, flags.FORCE_TXSTMIN will be set

type tx_stmin

int

rtype

```

methods.name: isotp.tpsock.socket.set_fc_opts
documentation:
Sets the flow control options of the socket. Values of `None` will leave the parameter unchanged

```
param bs

The block size sent in the flow control message. Indicates the number of consecutive frame a sender can send before the socket sends a new flow control. A block size of 0 means that no additional flow control message will be sent (block size of infinity)

type bs

int

param stmin

The minimum separation time sent in the flow control message. Indicates the amount of time to wait between 2 consecutive frame. This value will be sent as is over CAN. Values from 1 to 127 means milliseconds. Values from 0xF1 to 0xF9 means 100us to 900us. 0 Means no timing requirements

type stmin

int

param wftmax

Maximum number of wait frame (flow control message with flow status=1) allowed before dropping a message. 0 means that wait frame are not allowed

type wftmax

int

rtype

```

methods.name: isotp.tpsock.socket.bind
documentation:
Binds the socket to an address.

```
param interface

The network interface to use

type interface

string

param address

The address to bind to.

type address

```

A IsoTP socket wrapper for easy configuration

```
param timeout

Passed down to the socket settimeout method. Control the blocking/non-blocking behavior of the socket

type timeout

int | None
```

class_variables.name: isotp.address.TargetAddressType.Physical
documentation: 1 to 1 communication

class_variables.name: isotp.address.TargetAddressType.Functional
documentation: 1 to n communication

classes.name: isotp.address.Address
documentation:
Represents the addressing information (N_AI) of the IsoTP layer. Will define what messages will be received and how to craft transmitted message to reach a specific party.

Parameters must be given according to the addressing mode. When not needed, a parameter may be left unset or set to `None`.

Both the {class}`TransportLayer<isotp.TransportLayer>` and the {class}`isotp.socket<isotp.socket>` expects this address object

```
param addressing_mode

The addressing mode. Valid values are defined by the  class

type addressing_mode

int

param txid

The CAN ID for transmission. Used for these addressing mode: Normal_11bits, Normal_29bits, Extended_11bits, Extended_29bits, Mixed_11bits

type txid

int | None

param rxid

The CAN ID for reception. Used for these addressing mode: Normal_11bits, Normal_29bits, Extended_11bits, Extended_29bits, Mixed_11bits

type rxid

int | None

param target_address

Target address (N_TA) used in NormalFixed_29bits and Mixed_29bits addressing mode.

type target_address

int | None

param source_address

Source address (N_SA) used in NormalFixed_29bits and Mixed_29bits addressing mode.

type source_address

int | None

param physical_id

The CAN ID for physical (unicast) messages. Only bits 28-16 are used. Used for these addressing modes: NormalFixed_29bits, Mixed_29bits. Set to standard mandated value if None.

type physical_id

int | None

param functional_id

The CAN ID for functional (multicast) messages. Only bits 28-16 are used. Used for these addressing modes: NormalFixed_29bits, Mixed_29bits. Set to standard mandated value if None.

type functional_id

int | None

param address_extension

Address extension (N_AE) used in Mixed_11bits, Mixed_29bits addressing mode

type address_extension

int | None

param rx_only

When using , indicates that this address is the RX part, disabling validation of TX part

type rx_only

bool

param tx_only

When using , indicates that this address is the TX part, disabling validation of RX part

type tx_only

bool
```

classes.name: isotp.address.AsymmetricAddress
documentation:
Address that uses independent addressing modes for transmission and reception.

```
param tx_addr

The Address object used for transmission

type tx_addr

 with tx_only=True

param rx_addr

The Address object used for reception

type rx_addr

 with rx_only=True
```

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.send
documentation:
Enqueue an IsoTP frame to be sent over CAN network.
When performing a blocking send, this method returns only when the transmission is complete or raise an exception when a failure or a timeout occurs.
See {ref}`blocking_send<param_blocking_send>`

```
param data

The data to be sent. Can either be a bytearray or a tuple containing a generator and a size. The generator should return integer

type data

bytearray | (Generator, int)

param target_address_type

Optional parameter that can be Physical (0) for 1-to-1 communication or Functional (1) for 1-to-n.
See .
If not provided, parameter  will be used (default to Physical)

type target_address_type

int

param send_timeout

Timeout value for blocking send. Unused if  is False

type send_timeout

float or None

raises ValueError

Given data is not a bytearray, a tuple (generator,size) or the size is too big

raises RuntimeError

Transmit queue is full or tried to transmit while the stack is configured in 

raises BlockingSendTimeout

When  is set to True and the send operation does not complete in the given timeout.

raises BlockingSendFailure

When  is set to True and the transmission failed for any reason (e.g. unexpected frame or bad timings), including a timeout. Note that
 inherits .
```

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.recv
documentation:
Dequeue an IsoTP frame from the reception queue if available.

```
param block

Tells if the read should be blocking or not

type block

bool

param timeout

Timeout value used for blocking read only

type timeout

float

return

The next available IsoTP frame

rtype

bytearray or None
```
Receive an IsoTP frame. Output of the layer

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.available
documentation: Returns `True` if an IsoTP frame is awaiting in the reception queue. `False` otherwise

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.transmitting

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.process
documentation:
Function to be called periodically, as fast as possible.
This function is expected to block only if the given rxfn performs a blocking read.

```
param rx_timeout

Timeout for any read operation

type rx_timeout

float

param do_rx

Process reception when True

type do_rx

bool

param do_tx

Process transmission when True

type do_tx

bool

return

Statistics about what have been accomplished during the call

rtype

```

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic._set_rxfn
documentation:
Allow post init change of rxfn. This is a trick to implement the threaded Transport Layer
and keeping the ability to run the TransportLayerLogic without threads for backward compatibility
with v1.x

documentation: Process the reception of a CAN message. Moves the reception state machine accordingly and optionally

documentation: Process the transmit state machine

```
 or
```

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic._pad_message_data
documentation: Pad a message if required with the proper padding byte according to the configuration

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.stop_sending
documentation: Request the TransportLayer object to stop transmitting, clear the transmit buffer and put back its transmit state machine to idle state.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.stop_receiving
documentation:
Request the TransportLayer object to stop receiving, clear the reception buffer and put back its receive state machine to idle state. 
If a reception is ongoing, the following messages will be discared and considered like garbage

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic._start_reception_after_first_frame_if_valid
documentation: Init the reception of a multi-pdu frame.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.reset
documentation: Reset the layer: Empty all buffers, set the internal state machines to Idle
Clears everything within the layer.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.sleep_time
documentation:
Returns a value in seconds that can be passed to `time.sleep()` when the stack is processed in a different thread.

The value will change according to the internal state machine state, sleeping longer while idle and shorter when active.
Gives a time to pass to time.sleep() based on the state of the FSM. Avoid using too much CPU

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.is_tx_throttled
documentation: Tells if the transmission is actively being slowed down by the rate limited

inner_classes.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.SendRequest
documentation: An object representing a call to `TransportLayer.send() by the user. Wraps the given parameter and associate with a completion event and a success flag`

inner_classes.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayerLogic.ProcessStats
documentation: Some statistics produced by every `process` called indicating how much has been accomplish during that iteration.

classes.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayer

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayer.start
documentation: Starts the IsoTP layer. Starts internal threads that handle the IsoTP communication.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayer.stop
documentation: Stops the IsoTP layer. Stops the internal threads that handle the IsoTP communication and reset the layer state.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayer._relay_thread_fn
documentation: Internal function executed by the relay thread. Reads the user rxfn and put any results in a queue.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayer._main_thread_fn
documentation: Internal function executed by the main thread.

methods.name: isotp.protocol.TransportLayer.process
documentation: Protect against usage of non thread-safe methods while threads are running. We don't hide those method for backward compatibility

An IsoTP transport layer implementation that runs in a separate thread. The main public interface are `start`, `stop`, `send`, `recv`.
For backward compatibility, this class will behave similarly as a V1 TransportLayer if start/stop are never called; meaning that `process()` can be called by the user

```
param rxfn

Function to be called by the transport layer to read the CAN layer. Must return a  or None if no message has been received.
For optimal performance, this function should perform a blocking read that waits on IO

type rxfn

Callable : expected signature: my_rxfn(timeout:float) -> Optional[isotp.CanMessage]

param txfn

Function to be called by the transport layer to send a message on the CAN layer. This function should receive a 

type txfn

Callable : expected signature: my_txfn(msg:isotp.CanMessage) -> None

param address

The address information of CAN messages. Includes the addressing mode, txid/rxid, source/target address and address extension. See  for more details.

type address

isotp.Address

param error_handler

A function to be called when an error has been detected.
An  (inheriting Exception class) will be given as sole parameter. See the 
When started, the error handler will be called from a different thread than the user thread, make sure to consider thread safety if the error handler is more complex than a log.

type error_handler

Callable

param params

Dict of parameters for the transport layer. See 

type params

dict

param read_timeout

Default blocking read timeout passed down to the rxfn. Affects only the reading thread time granularity which can affect timing performance.
A value between 20ms-500ms should generally be good. MEaningless if the provided rxfn ignores its timeout parameter

type read_timeout

float
```
Inheritance of TransportLayerLogic instead of using composition is a design choice to ease backward compatibility at the expense of a more crowded interface.

classes.name: isotp.protocol.CanStack
documentation:
The IsoTP transport layer pre configured to use [python-can](https://python-can.readthedocs.io) as CAN layer. python-can must be installed in order to use this class.
All parameters except the `bus` parameter will be given to the {class}`TransportLayer<isotp.TransportLayer>` constructor

This class directly calls `bus.recv`, consuming the message from the receive queue, potentially starving other application. Consider using the {class}`NotifierBasedCanStack<isotp.NotifierBasedCanStack>`
to avoid starvation issues

```
param bus

A python-can bus object implementing recv and send

type bus

can.BusABC

param args

Passed down to . rxfn and txfn are predefined

type args

N/A

param kwargs

Passed down to . rxfn and txfn are predefined

type kwargs

N/A
```

classes.name: isotp.protocol.NotifierBasedCanStack
documentation:
The IsoTP transport layer pre configured to use [python-can](https://python-can.readthedocs.io) as CAN layer and reading through a `can.Notifier`. python-can must be installed in order to use this class.
All parameters except the `bus` and the `notifier` parameter will be given to the {class}`TransportLayer<isotp.TransportLayer>` constructor

This class reads by registering a listener to the given notifier and sends by calling `bus.recv`.

```
param bus

A python-can Bus object implementing send used for transmission

type bus

can.BusABC

param notifier

A python-can Notifier object onto which a new listener will be added

type notifier

can.Notifier

param args

Passed down to . rxfn and txfn are predefined

type args

N/A

param kwargs

Passed down to . rxfn and txfn are predefined

type kwargs

N/A
```
